# Global memory — loaded by Claude Code in EVERY session and EVERY repo
# Generated by install.py from templates/CLAUDE.global.md — do not edit this copy.

@memory.active.md

<!-- ^ Facts learned in past sessions. Auto-loaded, so they persist across
     session switches. Generated file — never edit by hand. -->

## AUTOMATIC MEMORY CAPTURE (act on this without being asked)

You have a persistent memory. Use it on your own initiative.

**When during a conversation I reveal a durable preference or fact about how I
work — e.g. I say "remember…", "from now on…", "always…", "I prefer…", or I
correct you in a generalizing way — immediately run, without asking permission:**

```{{CODE_FENCE}}
{{PYTHON_CMD}} "{{MEMORY}}" --global add "<concise fact, <200 chars>" --quote "<my words>"
```

For a durable fact about THIS project (tech stack, a flow/architecture decision,
an issue→root-cause→fix, a convention), capture it to the repo tier instead:

```{{CODE_FENCE}}
{{PYTHON_CMD}} "{{MEMORY}}" add "<concise fact>" --scope repo --quote "<evidence>"
```

Rules:
- One fact per call. Keep it concise and general.
- If it might already exist, run the same `add` anyway — duplicates auto-vote-up.
- Do NOT store: one-off/task-specific instructions, secrets, tokens, or personal
  sensitive data.
- After running it, tell me in one line what you saved.

This is how you remember me between sessions. Treat capturing my preferences as
part of doing the task well, not an interruption.

If you ever see a line beginning with `[memory-setup]` in your context, relay it
to me verbatim once — it's a one-time setup suggestion from the memory system.
